phases.name: Navigation and authority guardrails
phases.acceptanceGate: agent can classify active law, support, holding, and legacy material

phases.name: Platform module boundary map
phases.acceptanceGate: agent can scaffold runtime services without inventing CRUD boundaries

phases.name: Public application surface closure
phases.acceptanceGate: agent can identify callable public operations and forbidden internals

phases.name: Agent use, preflight, explain, and reason codes
phases.acceptanceGate: high-consequence operations return structured preflight/result/problem/trace posture

phases.name: Query/current-state/output app contracts
phases.acceptanceGate: apps cannot treat materializations, query results, or compiled output previews as truth stores; stale/disputed/redacted/evidence-insufficient states are preserved
artifacts: query cookbook and output preview examples
phases.researchIntake: deep-research-report-25.md reviewed; result qualification and preview-first output assembly adopted as implementation support

phases.name: Workflow cookbook and UI-safe state matrix
phases.acceptanceGate: agent-built apps preserve recommendation/prescription/work-order/claim/evidence/accepted-execution/correction/dispute/output states without unsafe labels

phases.name: Practical farm contracts
phases.acceptanceGate: offline/FMIS/calculation/identity flows do not rely on hidden app heuristics and candidate/minimum-capture states remain non-truth unless governed promotion succeeds
phases.researchIntake: deep-research-report-25.md reviewed; candidate-first sync/import, formula governance, source-fidelity/loss maps, and numeric determinism adopted as implementation support

phases.name: SDK/API bundle and reference skeleton
phases.acceptanceGate: agents implement from generated public clients and skeleton boundaries rather than inventing API or architecture; internal surfaces do not leak into SDKs
phases.researchIntake: deep-research-report-25.md reviewed; thin public-contract-generated SDKs, AsyncAPI listeners, public/internal boundary enforcement, and skeleton import boundaries adopted as implementation support

phases.name: Agent conformance and break tests
phases.acceptanceGate: semantic-law blockers, contract discipline, sync/import, numeric/display, explainability, SDK boundary, two-agent FMIS, and hard-path offline contractor tests pass before agent-ready runtime claims
phases.researchIntake: deep-research-report-25.md reviewed; conformance suite aimed at AI-agent shortcut failures adopted as implementation support

phases.name: Baseline harmonization readiness
phases.acceptanceGate: baseline harmonization proposals are explicit, runtime claims remain blocked, and no active baseline edits are applied
phases.researchIntake: deep-research-report-25.md used to distinguish promotion candidates, runtime evidence gates, and do-not-promote items
